query: Create your own Mac apps by chatting with AI alternative

results.title: Autopilot • Writing AI Chatbot - App Store - Apple
results.content: This is an OpenAI (chatGPT) implementation into macOS, and it lets you call up chatGPT from anywhere in within the OS, and just start chatting with it. It also gives you the ability to highlight any text, and apply any number of commands – many of which come in the app, like "explain it like I'm five", "tralslate to...," "fix grammar," etc.The power comes in being able to create your own templates and assign shortcuts to them.I've also found it really handy in combination with macOS's built-in speech-to-text input, which makes asking complex, conversational questions really easy.To top it all off, you can pair this with other AI apps, like text-to-speech, and voilá, you have a virtual AI assistant that you can speak to.Again, this is a simple app, but powerful nonetheless. Its just the [...] This is an OpenAI (chatGPT) implementation into macOS, and it lets you call up chatGPT from anywhere in within the OS, and just start chatting with it. It also gives you the ability to highlight any text, and apply any number of commands – many of which come in the app, like "explain it like I'm five", "tralslate to...," "fix grammar," etc.The power comes in being able to create your own templates and assign shortcuts to them.I've also found it really handy in combination with macOS's built-in speech-to-text input, which makes asking complex, conversational questions really easy.To top it all off, you can pair this with other AI apps, like text-to-speech, and voilá, you have a virtual AI assistant that you can speak to.Again, this is a simple app, but powerful nonetheless. Its just the [...] Autopilot is your personal writing assistant, teacher, and copilot. It integrates the power of AI and ChatGPT in all your Mac apps. It is also available inside Safari websites and Youtube videos.

results.title: The best AI apps for Mac you need right now
results.content: Olama supports a range of them, and you interact with them through their client. As a Mac user, I've been using the native Mac OS client called Olamac to chat with Olama locally, which runs Meta's Llama model. The interface feels a lot like chat GPT. Super clean and easy to use. I especially love the syntax highlighting for code, which is super helpful while learning to program. Lama does require an internet connection, so it's not going to work offline like Fluid does. It's also completely free and open- source, making it a great option for anybody looking for a flexible cross-platform AI assistant. One of the most helpful AI tools for learning to program for me has been Typing Mind. It's a multimodel AI platform with tons of specialized agents from lifestyle and wellness to design, [...] different plans available for specific features and an open AI API key is required. Let's explore some AI tools for working with documents. Starting with PDF Pals, a native Mac OS app designed to summarize PDFs. With PDF Pals, you can chat with AI to summarize key insights from long documents within seconds, saving you hours of reading. Now, unlike other PDF viewers, there's no size or page limits. And it even works with scan documents thanks to its OCR technology. Your files stay local on your Mac, keeping them secure. There's no cloud storage involved. It works with PDFs from emails or ones you download from the web. This is perfect for summarizing academic papers, technical details from manuals, reviewing legal documents, and much more. PDF Pals is especially useful for summarizing [...] requests being processed locally on your Mac while keeping your data private. Whether you're typing or speaking, it feels natural and responsive. As someone learning to program, I've found Fluid incredibly useful for understanding key components and languages in a distraction-free conversational environment. It also has a handy history mode so you can revisit past conversations. Fluid is free for Macs with Apple Silicon with a paid version that adds features like private AI access and more tools like memory and web search are coming soon. Another AI assistant worth checking out is Olama. It's like fluid, but it works on Mac OS, Windows, and Linux, so it's great for using on any platform. To get started, you install an AI model locally. Olama supports a range of them, and you interact with

results.title: What I Learned While Building a macOS App with GPT-4
results.content:
If you're about to start building app with chatGPT, and you have 0 knowledge in software development, I highly recommend taking a few days to study the basics first. There are many free resources to get started:

 Software Development: www.freecodecamp.org, www.codecademy.com
 Github: 
 APIs: 
 UX: 
 MVP Development: Lean Startup by Eric Ries
 User Testing and Feedback: www.usability.gov

## Good Questions Matters

In school, we’re rewarded for having the right answer, not for asking a good question. This will have to change entirely. Our brain is greatly limited compared to AI which has absorbed all the knowledge in the world. With AI by your side and ability to ask good questions, you’re one step ahead. [...] I'm usually taking an approach like describing the UI for a blind person. Explaining from top-down, left-right how each elements look like, what's the hover effect, how it looks like after interaction, etc.

Also adding dark theme to my app was a piece of cake.

### Small Functional blocks

AI is great companion if you need to create just one specific function.

Here’s a tip: have a chat with your AI about the layout and artchitecture of your app first. Once that’s sorted, ask it to writing all those individual functions for you one by one.

### All kind of time-saving scripts
